LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF VICTOR TONG'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT AND MOTION TO ALLOW FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DENIED. AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT "ESTABLISHED THAT THERE IS A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY" HE WILL PREVAIL. (CCP 425.13.) AS TO THE PROPOSED FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION DOES NOT MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1324(B). PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576

ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT TO CORRECT AND CLARIFY DEFENDANTS' NAME
ORDER SUSTAINING IN PART AND OVERRULING IN PART PLAINTIFF'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER DEFENDANT VINCENT LEUNG, DDS

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF VICTOR TONG'S DEMURRER TO DEFENDANT LEUNG'S ANSWER IS SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. THE DEMURRER TO THE 21ST ANSWER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE AS CURRENTLY PLED IT IS AMBIGUOUS AND DOES NOT IDENTIFY A SPECIFIC PORTION OF THE AFFORDABLE CARE ACT. THE DEMURRER TO THE REMAINING AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES IS OVERRULED BECAUSE THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES ARE FACTUALLY SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE DEFENSES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/JMQ)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF VICTOR TONG'S MOTION TO STRIKE LEUNG'S ANSWER IS DENIED AS MOOT IN PART AND DENIED ON THE MERITS IN PART. THE MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANT'S 21ST AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE IS RENDERED MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE CONCURRENTLY-DECIDED DEMURRER. THE 1ST, 2ND, 4TH, 5TH, 14TH, 15TH, 17TH-19TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES CANNOT BE STRICKEN BASED ON TONG'S CLAIMS THAT THEY ARE FALSE. STRIKING THEM ON SUCH GROUNDS WOULD REQUIRE THE COURT TO RESOLVE DISPUTES OF FACT (TONG'S ALLEGATIONS VS. LEUNG'S DENIALS), WHICH IS IMPROPER AT THIS STAGE; IN ANY EVENT, THERE EXISTS NO EVIDENTIARY BASIS THAT WOULD ALLOW THE COURT TO RESOLVE ANY FACTUAL DISPUTES REGARDING THE TRUTH OR FALSITY OF FACTS RELIED UPON BY LEUNG. THE 3RD, 4TH, 6TH, 12TH-16TH, 17TH, AND 20TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES MAY DEPEND ON FACTS BEYOND THOSE RAISED BY TONG, BUT THEY ARE NOT REQUESTS FOR AFFIRMATIVE RELIEF AND, THEREFORE, TONG'S MOTION ON SUCH GROUNDS MUST FAIL. (SEE CITY OF STOCKTON V. SUPERIOR COURT (2007) 42 CAL.4TH 730, 745-746 F. 12.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/JMQ)

ORDER ON PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANTS
ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSER OF DEFENDANT VINCENT LEUNG, D.D.S.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF VICTOR TONG'S MOTION TO STRIKE TIN'S ANSWER IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE 23RD AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE IS GRANTED BECAUSE CCP SECTION 411.35 DEALS WITH ARCHITECTS, NOT DENTAL PROFESSIONALS. THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE 25TH AND 26TH ANSWERS IS DENIED BECAUSE THOSE DEFENSES DO NOT SEEK AFFIRMATIVE RELIEF. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE 1ST-3RD, 5TH-9TH, 11TH-13TH, 15TH, 16TH AND 20TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES ON THE GROUNDS OF UNCERTAINTY OR INSUFFICIENT FACTS IS DENIED BECAUSE DEFENSES CAN BE STRICKEN ONLY IF IRRELEVANT, FALSE, IMPROPER, OR IN ORDER TO REMOVE ANY PART OF THE PLEADING NOT DRAWN OR FILED IN CONFORMITY WITH THE LAWS OF THE STATE, A COURT RULE, OR AN ORDER OF THE COURT. (CCP SECTION 436.) THE 1ST, 6TH, 13TH, 16TH, 21ST, AND 22ND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES CANNOT BE STRICKEN BASED ON TONG'S CLAIMS THAT THEY ARE FALSE. STRIKING THEM ON SUCH GROUNDS WOULD REQUIRE THE COURT TO RESOLVE DISPUTES OF FACT (TONG'S ALLEGATIONS VS. TIN'S DENIALS), WHICH IS IMPROPER AT THIS STAGE; IN ANY EVENT, THERE EXISTS NO EVIDENTIARY BASIS THAT WOULD ALLOW THE COURT TO RESOLVE ANY FACTUAL DISPUTES REGARDING THE TRUTH OR FALSITY OF FACTS RELIED UPON BY TIN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/JMQ)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF VICTOR TONG'S MOTION TO STRIKE REDWOOD DENTAL'S ANSWER IS DENIED. THE MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANT'S ANSWERS ON THE GROUNDS THAT THE ANSWERS DO NOT ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS OR ARE AMBIGUOUS MUST BE DENIED BECAUSE THESE ARE NOT PROPER GROUNDS FOR A MOTION TO STRIKE. A MOTION TO STRIKE CAN BE GRANTED IN ORDER TO STRIKE IRRELEVANT, FALSE, OR IMPROPER MATTER INSERTED IN THE PLEADING OR IN ORDER TO REMOVE ANY PART OF THE PLEADING NOT DRAWN OR FILED IN CONFORMITY WITH THE LAWS OF THE STATE, A COURT RULE, OR AN ORDER OF THE COURT. (CCP SECTION 436.) ON THIS RECORD, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS THAT THE 1ST, 2ND, 4TH, 5TH, 14TH, 15TH, 17TH-19TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES ARE FALSE WOULD REQUIRE THE COURT TO RESOLVE DISPUTES OF FACT (TONG'S ALLEGATIONS VS. REDWOOD DENTAL'S DENIALS), WHICH IS IMPROPER AT THIS STAGE; IN ANY EVENT, THERE EXISTS NO EVIDENTIARY BASIS THAT WOULD ALLOW THE COURT TO RESOLVE ANY FACTUAL DISPUTES REGARDING THE TRUTH OR FALSITY OF FACTS RELIED UPON BY REDWOOD DENTAL. THE 3RD, 4TH, 6TH-8TH, 12TH-16TH, 17TH, AND 20TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES MAY DEPEND ON FACTS BEYOND THOSE RAISED BY TONG, BUT THEY ARE NOT REQUESTS FOR AFFIRMATIVE RELIEF AND, THEREFORE, TONG'S MOTION ON SUCH GROUNDS MUST FAIL. (SEE CITY OF STOCKTON V. SUPERIOR COURT (2007) 42 CAL.4TH 730, 745-746 F. 12.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/JMQ)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend The Second Amended Complaint And Motion To Allow For Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Aug-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Victor Tong (415-760-3752) appears as plaintiff, In Propria Persona

Attorney, Norman La Force of Law Offices of Christian B. Green (415-932-7465) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Vincent Leung DDS

Attorney, Benson C. Lai, Esq. (415-806-8088) appears as counsel for defendant, Tind Sunset Primer

The cause is argued and submitted by all sides present. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF VICTOR TONG'S Motion For Leave To Amend The Second Amended Complaint And Motion To Allow For Punitive Damages is DENIED. As to punitive damages, plaintiff has not "established that there is a substantial probability" he will prevail. (CCP 425.13.) As to the proposed fraud cause of action, plaintiff's declaration does not meet the requirements of California Rule of Court 3.1324(b). Prevailing party to submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff VICTOR TONG, Tel No. 415-760-3752 appearing in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Matter heard at 2:00 p.m. Matter not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF VICTOR TONG'S Motion to Strike Tin's Answer is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The motion to strike the 23rd affirmative defense is granted because CCP section 411.35 deals with architects, not dental professionals. The motion to strike the 25th and 26th answers is denied because those defenses do not seek affirmative relief. Plaintiff's motion to strike the 1st-3rd, 5th-9th, 11th-13th, 15th, 16th and 20th affirmative defenses on the grounds of uncertainty or insufficient facts is denied because defenses can be stricken only if irrelevant, false, improper, or in order to remove any part of the pleading not drawn or filed in conformity with the laws of the state, a court rule, or an order of the court. (CCP section 436.) The 1st, 6th, 13th, 16th, 21st, and 22nd affirmative defenses cannot be stricken based on Tong's claims that they are false. Striking them on such grounds would require the court to resolve disputes of fact (Tong's allegations vs. Tin's denials), which is improper at this stage; in any event, there exists no evidentiary basis that would allow the court to resolve any factual disputes regarding the truth or falsity of facts relied upon by Tin. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Answer
Off Calendar Apr-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff VICTOR TONG, Tel No. 415-760-3752 appearing in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Matter heard at 2:00 p.m. Matter not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF VICTOR TONG'S Motion to Strike Leung's Answer is DENIED AS MOOT IN PART AND DENIED ON THE MERITS IN PART. The motion to strike Defendant's 21st affirmative defense is rendered moot in light of the ruling on the concurrently-decided demurrer. The 1st, 2nd, 4th, 5th, 14th, 15th, 17th-19th affirmative defenses cannot be stricken based on Tong's claims that they are false. Striking them on such grounds would require the court to resolve disputes of fact (Tong's allegations vs. Leung's denials), which is improper at this stage; in any event, there exists no evidentiary basis that would allow the court to resolve any factual disputes regarding the truth or falsity of facts relied upon by Leung. The 3rd, 4th, 6th, 12th-16th, 17th, and 20th affirmative defenses may depend on facts beyond those raised by Tong, but they are not requests for affirmative relief and, therefore, Tong's motion on such grounds must fail. (See City of Stockton v. Superior Court (2007) 42 Cal.4th 730, 745-746 f. 12.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff VICTOR TONG, Tel No. 415-760-3752 appearing in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Matter heard at 2:00 p.m. Matter not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF VICTOR TONG'S Demurrer to Defendant Leung's Answer is SUSTAINED WITH FIFTEEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. The demurrer to the 21st answer is sustained with leave to amend because as currently pled it is ambiguous and does not identify a specific portion of the Affordable Care Act. The demurrer to the remaining affirmative defenses is overruled because the affirmative defenses are factually sufficient to constitute defenses. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Joseph M. MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff VICTOR TONG, Tel No. 415-760-3752 appearing in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Matter heard at 2:00 p.m. Matter not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF VICTOR TONG'S Motion to Strike Redwood Dental's Answer is DENIED. The motion to strike Defendant's answers on the grounds that the answers do not allege sufficient facts or are ambiguous must be denied because these are not proper grounds for a motion to strike. A motion to strike can be granted in order to strike irrelevant, false, or improper matter inserted in the pleading or in order to remove any part of the pleading not drawn or filed in conformity with the laws of the state, a court rule, or an order of the court. (CCP section 436.) On this record, Plaintiff's claims that the 1st, 2nd, 4th, 5th, 14th, 15th, 17th-19th affirmative defenses are false would require the court to resolve disputes of fact (Tong's allegations vs. Redwood Dental's denials), which is improper at this stage; in any event, there exists no evidentiary basis that would allow the court to resolve any factual disputes regarding the truth or falsity of facts relied upon by Redwood Dental. The 3rd, 4th, 6th-8th, 12th-16th, 17th, and 20th affirmative defenses may depend on facts beyond those raised by Tong, but they are not requests for affirmative relief and, therefore, Tong's motion on such grounds must fail. (See City of Stockton v. Superior Court (2007) 42 Cal.4th 730, 745-746 f. 12.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/JMQ)
